title: Generations Dash gets families out and about at Groenkloof
description: GEORGE NEWS - Friends and family of all...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - Friends and family of all ages took part in the third annual Generations Dash at the Groenkloof Retirement Estate on Saturday morning, 29 March.


> The event, that attracted some 300 participants, was organised in collaboration with CAW Bio Biokineticists.


The idea was to get the Groenkloof residents and their families out into the sun and on the move.

It was hugely enjoyed by all and was concluded with a braai and social get-together at The Hub House, the coffee shop at Groenkloof.

VIEW A PHOTO GALLERY: Generations Dash attracts a large crowd

Sandy and Peter Lupton-Smith were joined by their daughter, Gill, for the walk.

Neighbours Vida Bosman and Annette Boshoff taking a rest after the Dash.

From left are Drs Ashleigh Merry and Migael van Velder, Ans Keyter and Jeanita le Roux, all from Easy Care that had a stall at the Dash.

Philip Weideman, Protea swimmer from George, and his mom Kobie enjoy a cooldrink after the walk.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
